data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260508T011138-e506f35c/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260508T011138-e506f35c/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260508T011138-e506f35c/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260508T011138-e506f35c/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260508T011138-e506f35c/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-f6fd75e7">
    <title>server: propagate main-worktree safety errors from pre-claim hook</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The server-managed worker pre-claim hook still treats every `FetchOriginAncestryCheck` error as an ignorable fetch failure. After the main-worktree safety changes, that call can also fail because the main checkout is on the wrong branch, has staged changes, or is otherwise unsafe for a claim. Swallowing those local safety errors lets server workers claim beads while the coordinator knows the main tree is not safe.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:1617-1628 calls `gitOps.FetchOriginAncestryCheck(projectRoot, branch)` and returns nil for all errors with the comment `Fetch failure is non-fatal`.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:677-691 now runs `FetchOriginAncestryCheck` under the main git lock and calls `ensureLandingWorktreeReady` before remote fetch, so not every error from that method is a remote fetch failure.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1970-1975 already distinguishes local safety errors from `git fetch origin` errors in the CLI pre-claim hook, so the server path is inconsistent.

PROPOSED FIX
- Share or duplicate a small error-classification helper so both CLI and server pre-claim hooks ignore only actual `git fetch origin` failures.
- Propagate local main-worktree safety errors from the server hook, including staged changes, uncommitted tracked changes, unexpected branch, detached HEAD where unsafe, and local git lock/readiness failures.
- Keep the air-gap behavior: a genuine origin fetch failure remains non-fatal and only skips the ancestry check.
- Add tests at the server hook boundary; do not rely only on CLI hook tests.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the land/merge locking implementation.
- Do not make remote fetch mandatory in air-gapped/offline projects.
- Do not change queue ranking or bead claim semantics beyond blocking unsafe local pre-claim state.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-5cb6e6cd. No deps.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestServerPreClaimHook_PropagatesStagedMainWorktreeError proves the server hook returns a local readiness error from FetchOriginAncestryCheck instead of swallowing it.
2. TestServerPreClaimHook_IgnoresGitFetchOriginFailure proves a genuine `git fetch origin` failure remains non-fatal.
3. TestCLIServerPreClaimHook_ErrorPolicyParity proves the CLI and server hooks apply the same fetch-vs-local-safety classification.
4. A shared helper or equivalent test fixture prevents the CLI and server policies from drifting again.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/... -run "TestServerPreClaimHook_PropagatesStagedMainWorktreeError|TestServerPreClaimHook_IgnoresGitFetchOriginFailure|TestCLIServerPreClaimHook_ErrorPolicyParity" -count=1 passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:server, area:agent, area:git, kind:bug, reliability, prevention, spec:FEAT-010</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-5cb6e6cd" base-rev="e841bd8e0aa1987d06b6c8c92c9f91653e217753" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260508T011138-e506f35c"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>
